q.en: According to the book, who were the Huns and where did they come from?
q.zh: 根据本书，匈人是谁？他们来自哪里？

options.en: Sailors from Britain
options.zh: 来自不列颠的水手

options.en: Traders from Egypt
options.zh: 来自埃及的商人

options.en: Farmers from the city of Rome according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 来自罗马城的农民（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: Ferocious horsemen from northcentral Asia
options.zh: 来自中亚的凶猛骑兵

explain.en: The book describes the Huns as ferocious horsemen from northcentral Asia who invaded Europe in the AD 370s.
explain.zh: 书中把匈人描述成来自中亚的凶猛骑兵，于公元370年代侵入欧洲。

q.en: Europeans found Attila so horrifying that they gave him a fearful nickname. What was it?
q.zh: 欧洲人觉得阿提拉太可怕，给他起了个可怕的绰号。是什么？

options.en: 'The Scourge of God'
options.zh: '上帝之鞭'

options.en: 'The Builder of Rome'
options.zh: '罗马的建设者'

options.en: 'The Sea Lord'
options.zh: '海洋之主'

options.en: 'The Friendly King'
options.zh: '友善之王'

explain.en: The people of Europe called Attila 'the Scourge of God' because he crushed nearly every army sent against him.
explain.zh: 欧洲人称阿提拉为'上帝之鞭'，因为他几乎击溃了所有讨伐他的军队。

q.en: The book shows maps of the Roman Empire at different dates. Why?
q.zh: 书中展示了不同年代的罗马帝国地图。为什么？

options.en: To help readers see how the empire changed as the Huns attacked
options.zh: 为了帮助读者看清帝国在匈人进攻下如何变化

options.en: To show where Attila was born according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 为了标示阿提拉的出生地（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: To explain how to build roads
options.zh: 为了解释如何修筑道路

options.en: To list all Roman emperors
options.zh: 为了列出所有罗马皇帝

explain.en: Maps show the empire's divisions and territories, giving context to Attila's campaigns.
explain.zh: 地图呈现帝国的分裂与疆域，为阿提拉的征战提供背景。

q.en: Honoria, sister of the Western emperor, sent Attila a letter and her ring. How did Attila use it?
q.zh: 西罗马皇帝之妹霍诺莉娅给阿提拉寄了信和戒指。阿提拉如何利用它？

options.en: He returned it politely to her brother according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他礼貌地把它还给了她哥哥（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: He claimed it was a marriage proposal and demanded half the empire as dowry
options.zh: 他声称那是求婚，并要求把帝国一半作为嫁妆

options.en: He used it to make peace with Rome
options.zh: 他用它同罗马讲和

options.en: He burned it as a warning
options.zh: 他把它烧掉以作警告

explain.en: Attila announced the letter and ring were a proposal of marriage and demanded half the Western Empire as dowry.
explain.zh: 阿提拉宣称信和戒指是求婚，并要求把西罗马帝国一半作为嫁妆。

q.en: Who was the Roman general Aetius, and what nickname did he earn?
q.zh: 罗马将军埃提乌斯是谁？他获得了什么称号？

options.en: A Hun leader called 'the Scourge' according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 一位叫'上帝之鞭'的匈人首领（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: The emperor of the East
options.zh: 东罗马的皇帝

options.en: A pope who met Attila
options.zh: 一位会见阿提拉的教皇

options.en: A great general called 'the Last of the Romans'
options.zh: 一位被称为'最后的罗马人'的伟大将军

explain.en: Flavius Aetius served Valentinian III and was remembered as 'the Last of the Romans' for holding the empire together.
explain.zh: 弗拉维乌斯·埃提乌斯效力于瓦伦提尼安三世，因维系帝国而被誉为'最后的罗马人'。

q.en: The author gives a detailed account of the Battle of Chalons. Why?
q.zh: 作者详细描写了沙隆之战。为什么？

options.en: To explain how to forge a sword according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 为了讲解如何锻造宝剑（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: To describe Attila's wedding
options.zh: 为了描写阿提拉的婚礼

options.en: To show the moment Attila was finally defeated by Roman and Visigoth forces
options.zh: 为了展现阿提拉最终被罗马与西哥特联军击败的时刻

options.en: To list every Roman emperor
options.zh: 为了列出每位罗马皇帝

explain.en: The battle was one of history's greatest; Aetius's army defeated Attila there, a key turning point.
explain.zh: 沙隆之战是历史上最伟大的战役之一，埃提乌斯的军队在此击败阿提拉，是关键转折。

q.en: When Attila invaded Italy, what did Pope Leo I do?
q.zh: 阿提拉入侵意大利时，教皇利奥一世做了什么？

options.en: He fled to France
options.zh: 他逃往法国

options.en: He led an army into battle
options.zh: 他亲自领兵作战

options.en: He met Attila and persuaded him to leave Italy
options.zh: 他会见阿提拉并说服他离开意大利

options.en: He paid Attila all the gold according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他把所有黄金都给了阿提拉（按书中另一种说法）

explain.en: Aetius and Valentinian asked Pope Leo I to meet Attila; Attila agreed to stop his attacks on Italy.
explain.zh: 埃提乌斯和瓦伦提尼安请教皇利奥一世会见阿提拉，阿提拉同意停止进攻意大利。

q.en: How does the book explain the death of Attila in AD 453?
q.zh: 本书如何解释公元453年阿提拉的死？

options.en: He was killed in battle by Aetius according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他在战斗中被埃提乌斯杀死（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: He suffered a burst blood vessel and drowned in his own blood on his wedding night
options.zh: 他在新婚之夜血管破裂，溺死在自己的血中

options.en: He died of old age in Rome
options.zh: 他在罗马安老而终

options.en: He was poisoned by his cook
options.zh: 他被厨师下毒害死

explain.en: After marrying Ildico, Attila burst a blood vessel in his nose or throat and drowned in his own blood.
explain.zh: 娶了伊尔迪科后，阿提拉鼻或喉部血管破裂，溺死在自己的血中。

q.en: After Attila's death, what became of the Huns' empire?
q.zh: 阿提拉死后，匈人帝国变成了什么样子？

options.en: They built the city of Budapest
options.zh: 他们建造了布达佩斯城

options.en: His sons split it, they grew weak, and Germanic tribes overthrew them
options.zh: 他的儿子瓜分了帝国，势力衰弱，日耳曼部落将其推翻

options.en: They became allies of Rome forever according to some versions of the story
options.zh: 他们永远成了罗马的盟友（按书中另一种说法）

options.en: They conquered all of Europe
options.zh: 他们征服了整个欧洲

explain.en: Divided among his sons, the Huns weakened and were overthrown by Germanic tribes around 454, 'scattered to the winds.'
explain.zh: 帝国被其子瓜分后衰败，约454年被日耳曼部落推翻，'随风四散'。

q.en: What overall point does the book make about Attila and the Huns?
q.zh: 本书关于阿提拉和匈人传达了什么总体观点？

options.en: They were a terrifying force, but division after Attila ended their power
options.zh: 他们曾是可怕的势力，但阿提拉死后内部分裂终结了他们的力量

options.en: They built the Roman Empire according to some versions of the story in an early draft of the book
options.zh: 他们建立了罗马帝国（按书中另一种说法）（在书的早期草稿中）

options.en: They were peaceful farmers
options.zh: 他们是和平的农民

options.en: They never threatened Rome
options.zh: 他们从未威胁过罗马

explain.en: The book shows the Huns as a great terror who faded once disunity followed Attila's death.
explain.zh: 本书把匈人描绘成巨大威胁，而在阿提拉死后因内部分裂而衰亡。

prompt.en: Attila was called 'the Scourge of God.' Do you think one powerful leader can change history alone? Use examples from the book to support your view.
prompt.zh: 阿提拉被称为'上帝之鞭'。你认为一位强有力领袖能单独改变历史吗？用书中例子支持你的看法。

en: Think about how Attila united the Huns but division destroyed them.
Consider the roles of Aetius, the Visigoths, and Pope Leo I.
zh: 想想阿提拉如何统一匈人，而内部分裂又如何毁掉他们。
想想埃提乌斯、西哥特人和教皇利奥一世各自扮演的角色。
